zh.lifeSummary: 當Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II於大約1138年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-吉馬內達馬-素-迪奧時，他的父親Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton29歲，母親Clemence de Bar29歲。他於1166年與Mahaut Matilde de Clermont在英國巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1200年9月19日在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利利勒波內享年63歲，埋葬在法國皮卡迪奧易沙吉洛-德也瑟倫特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II于大约1138年出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-吉馬內達馬-素-迪奧时，他的父亲Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton29岁，母亲Clemence de Bar29岁。他于1166年与Mahaut Matilde de Clermont在英国巴金哈姆爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1200年9月19日在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利利勒波內享年63岁，埋葬在法国皮卡迪奧易沙吉洛-德也瑟倫特。

nameMeanings.name: Sieur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Monique.
French: variant of Ségur (see Segur ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Segur
meanings: English: altered form of   Segar  .

French (southern; Ségur) and Catalan: habitational name from any of various places in the southern part of France and in Catalonia named with Occitan ségur, Catalan segur ‘safe, well-defended’. Compare Sigur .
Spanish (Extremadura): nickname or metonymic occupational name from segur ‘small axe’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bandt
meanings: German: variant of   Band  .

variants.name: Bandstra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Wouter.
West Frisian: habitational name composed with Band 4 + the suffix -stra from Old Frisian sittera ‘inhabitant of’.

variants.name: Banta
meanings: West Frisian: probably a habitational name for someone from Bant, in the 17th century an island in Friesland, now a village north of Emmeloord in the Noordoostpolder (see   Band   4, compare   Banda   5). This surname is no longer found in the Netherlands.
History: Epke Jacobs Banta arrived in New Netherland in 1659.

variants.name: Bandel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Zvi, Emanuel, Hyman, Meir.
South German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from a diminutive of   Band  . See also   Bendel  .
German: in some cases, a variant of Pandel, a pet form of the medieval personal name Pantaleon (see Pantaleo ).

variants.name: Bend
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire): from Middle English bend ‘band, fetter, chain; ribbon, sash, stripe’, for someone who made, wore, or used such items. See also the Band 1 and compare Bendy 1.

variants.name: Bandler
meanings: German: occupational name for a maker of band (for vessels) or ribbon or lace, from an agent derivative of Middle High German bandel, a diminutive of bant ‘band’ (see Band ).

variants.name: Bandy
meanings: English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandi, bondi, bendi, derivatives of Middle English and Old English band, bond, bend ‘band, ribbon, stripe’ + Middle English -i, reinforced by Old French bendé ‘striped’, with reference perhaps to clothing ornamented with ribbons or stripes, or to the diagonal band of a heraldic device. Compare Band .
English: alternatively, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandy, bendy ‘curved stick’, used for someone with bent or crooked legs.
Czech and Slovak: from a derivative of banda ‘(gipsy) band’, apparently a nickname for a bandsman. This surname is found among Gypsies.

variants.name: Bond
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

nameMeanings.name: Mello
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Adelino, Ana, Joaquin, Acacio, Claudio, Cristina, Fernando, Americo, Enrique.  Portuguese Duarte, Joaquim, Armanda, Joao, Paulo, Serafim.
Italian: probably from a short form of a personal name ending with the hypocoristic suffix -ello, such as Giacomello from Giacomo .
Portuguese: variant of   Melo  .

variants.name: Giacomo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenico, Gino, Salvatore.
Italian: from the personal name Giacomo, from Latin Jacobus via Late Latin Jacomus (see James and Jacob ).

variants.name: Melle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Horst, Kurt, Otto.
Italian: habitational name from Melle in Cuneo province (Piedmont); in some cases possibly also a variant of a personal name such as   Mello   1.
Norwegian: probably a variant of   Mele  .

variants.name: Mellone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Emidio, Luigi, Pasquale.
Italian: from an augmentative of   Mello  .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Aubry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Aubrey .

en.lifeSummary: When Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II was born about 1138, in Dammartin-sur-Tigeaux, Seine-et-Marne, Île-de-France, France, his father, Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton, was 29 and his mother, Clemence de Bar, was 29. He married Mahaut Matilde de Clermont in 1166, in Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 19 September 1200, in Lillebonne, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, at the age of 63, and was buried in Saint-Leu-d'Esserent, Oise, Picardie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II est né environ 1138, à Dammartin-sur-Tigeaux, Seine-et-Marne, Île-de-France, France, son père, Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Clemence de Bar, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Mahaut Matilde de Clermont en 1166, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 19 septembre 1200, à Lillebonne, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Saint-Leu-d'Esserent, Oise, Picardie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II um 1138 in Dammartin-sur-Tigeaux, Seine-et-Marne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton 29 und seine Mutter Clemence de Bar 29. Er hat 1166 in Buckinghamshire, England Mahaut Matilde de Clermont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 19. September 1200 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Lillebonne, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Saint-Leu-d'Esserent, Oise, Picardie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II è nato il circa 1138 nel Dammartin-sur-Tigeaux, Seine-et-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Clemence de Bar, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Mahaut Matilde de Clermont nel 1166, nel Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 19 settembre 1200, nel Lillebonne, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Saint-Leu-d'Esserent, Oise, Picardie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello IIが約1138年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスセアイネ-エト-マルンダンマルティン-スル-ティジェアウックスで生まれた時，父Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Nortonは29歳，母Clemence de Barは29歳であった。1166年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアでMahaut Matilde de Clermontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1200年9月19日にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメリレボンで63歳で死去し，フランスピカーディオイゼサイント-レウ-ド'エセレントにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II(이)가 대략 1138년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 세이인-트-마니 다마틴-수-티그이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Clemence de Bar(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1166년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 Mahaut Matilde de Clermont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1200년 9월 19일 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 릴레보니에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 피카디 이스 세인트-레우-드'세렌트에 묻혔다.

mn.Sibthorpe, Nottinghamshire,England: Sibthorpe, Snotengaham, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II nasceu aproximadamente 1138, em Dammartin-sur-Tigeaux, Seine-et-Marne, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Clemence de Bar, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Mahaut Matilde de Clermont em 1166, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de setembro de 1200, em Lillebonne, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Saint-Leu-d'Esserent, Oise, Picardie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II родился около 1138 г. в местности даммартин-сур-тигокс, сине-ет-марн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Clemence de Bar, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Mahaut Matilde de Clermont в 1166 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 19 сентября 1200 г. в местности лиллебонн, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт-леу-д'ессирент, оис, пикарди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Aubry Sieur of Lillebonne and Mello II nació aproximadamente 1138, en Dammartin-sur-Tigeaux, Seine-et-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Comte Alberic I De Dammartin, Lord of Norton, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Clemence de Bar, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Mahaut Matilde de Clermont en 1166, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 19 de septiembre de 1200, en Lillebonne, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Saint-Leu-d'Esserent, Oise, Picardie, Francia.
